perturbation.original_text: Before doing so, each Party must ensure that affiliates and Representatives are required to protect the Confidential Information on terms consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Before doing so, each Party will make a good faith effort to ensure that affiliates and Representatives are required to protect the Confidential Information on terms consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by weakening the obligation to ensure affiliates and representatives protect confidential information. Replacing 'must ensure' with 'will make a good faith effort' lowers the standard of care required. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of confidentiality obligations, as a 'good faith effort' is subjective and could be interpreted differently, potentially leading to disputes. The original text had a strong direction but this one is giving the option to not make a full commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Security precautions . Each Party agrees: To take reasonable steps to protect the other Party’s Confidential Information. These steps must be at least as protective as those we take to protect our own Confidential Information of a similar nature ; To notify the other promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information; and Attachment A Page 3 of 5 To cooperate with the other to help regain control of the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized use or disclosure of it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation of each party to ensure affiliates and representatives protect confidential information by changing 'must ensure' to 'will make a good faith effort'. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that each party agrees 'to take reasonable steps to protect the other Party’s Confidential Information. These steps must be at least as protective as those we take to protect our own Confidential Information of a similar nature'. The 'good faith effort' standard is less stringent than 'reasonable steps...at least as protective', creating an inconsistency in the level of protection required, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party is required to restrict work assignments of Representatives who have had access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party is required to restrict work assignments of Representatives who have had access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by directly reversing the statement about restricting work assignments. The original text explicitly states that parties are NOT required to restrict assignments, while the changed text mandates it. This creates significant uncertainty and potential legal disputes regarding the handling of Representatives who have been exposed to confidential data. It directly goes against the other sections that provide how confidential information should be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sharing Confidential Information with affiliates and Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, a “Representative” is an employee, contractor, vendor, service provider, advisor or consultant of a Party or a Party’s respective affiliates including, but not limited to, Science Applications International Corporation ( “SAIC”) and Atos Governmental IT Outsourcing Services, LLC (“Atos”) and each of their respective employees , affiliates , and subcontractors . Each Party may disclose the other’s Confidential Information to its Representatives (who may then disclose that Co nfidential Information to other of its Representatives) only if those Representatives are required to have the Confidential Information for the purpose s in this Agreement. Before doing so, each Party must ensure that affiliates and Representatives are required to protect the Confidential Information on terms consistent with this Agreement. Each Party is required to restrict work assignments of Representatives who have had access to Confidential Information. Each Party acknowledges that they cannot control the incoming information the other Party will disclose in the course of the business relationship between the Parties , or what each Party’s Representatives will remember, even without notes or other aids. Each Party agrees that use of information in its Representatives’ unaided memories in the development or deployment of the Parties’ respective products or services does not create liability for that Party under this Agreement or trade secret law. Both Parties mutually agree to limit what each Party discloses to the other accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates that each party *is* required to restrict work assignments of representatives who have had access to Confidential Information, while the original text stated they were *not* required to do so. The contradicted text contains the changed text. This direct reversal of obligation creates a clear contradiction within the document regarding the handling of representatives with access to confidential information, as the changed text alters the original meaning found in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The five-year time period does not apply if applicable law requires a longer period.
perturbation.changed_text: The five-year time period does not apply regardless if applicable law requires a longer period.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause acknowledges that a longer period may apply if required by law. The changed text implies the five-year limit is firm even if the law dictates otherwise. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about the actual duration of confidentiality obligations. The parties won't know if they should apply 5 years or whatever the applicable law indicates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Length of Confidential Information obligations. Except as permitted above and unless otherwise required by applicable law or court order , neither Party will use or disclose the other’s Confidential Information for five years after it is received . The five-year time period does not apply regardless if applicable law requires a longer period. This duty to withhold Confidential Information survives any expiration or termination of this Agreement as provided herein .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the clause regarding the length of confidential information obligations. The original text stated that the five-year period doesn't apply if applicable law requires a longer period. The changed text states the five-year period does not apply 'regardless' if applicable law requires a longer period, which directly contradicts the phrase 'unless otherwise required by applicable law or court order' in the contradicted text. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each of us agrees that the other may seek court orders to stop Confidential Information from becoming public in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of us agrees that the other may NOT seek court orders to stop Confidential Information from becoming public in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by negating the right to seek court orders to prevent the disclosure of Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction with the intent of the whole document because it protects Confidential Information from being disclosed to third parties. The agreement goes against itself, as well, creates uncertainty about legal recourses in case of breach and contradicts the overall purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Security precautions . Each Party agrees: To take reasonable steps to protect the other Party’s Confidential Information. These steps must be at least as protective as those we take to protect our own Confidential Information of a similar nature ; To notify the other promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information; and Attachment A Page 3 of 5 To cooperate with the other to help regain control of the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized use or disclosure of it.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed seeking court orders to prevent disclosure of confidential information, whereas the changed text explicitly prohibits seeking such orders. The contradicted text requires parties to take reasonable steps to protect Confidential Information, notify the other party of unauthorized use, and cooperate to regain control and prevent further disclosure. By negating the ability to seek court orders, the changed text directly undermines the ability to effectively protect confidential information and prevent its disclosure, creating a strong contradiction. The changed text makes it harder to cooperate in regaining control of confidential information as laid out in the contradicted text.
